Title: Multi-level engineering facilitates the production of phenylpropanoid compounds in tomato

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly in fruit peel; not expressed in flesh
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates expression of genes involved in primary and secondary metabolism associated with flavonoid accumulation in tomato peel
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR binding to SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 promoters in WT peel; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB12 is the functional tomato homologue of AtMYB12. 2. SlMYB12 binds the same promoter motifs in SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 as AtMYB12. 3. SlMYB12 expression in peel is associated with elevated expression of primary- and flavonoid-pathway genes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly to show conservation of regulatory targets with AtMYB12; not the main transgene engineered in most experiments.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endogenous regulator for fruit peel flavonoid enhancement.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary polyphenols with potential health-promoting properties
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening; peel versus flesh comparison

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycolysis linked to shikimate/phenylpropanoid precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive polyphenols with nutritional and health-related value
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Induced in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in WT peel relative to flesh
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes precursor supply for aromatic amino acid and downstream phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; isotope-label flux analysis by transgenic AtMYB12 activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlENO transcript levels are increased in AtMYB12 tomato fruit. 2. AtMYB12 directly binds the SlENO promoter. 3. SlMYB12 also binds the same promoter region in WT peel. 4. Activation of SlENO is associated with increased carbon flux through glycolysis and enhanced phenylpropanoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key gene linking primary metabolism to aromatic amino acid/secondary metabolism supply
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening; AtMYB12 expression; glucose isotope feeding
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted by authors as one of the primary-metabolism genes directly activated by AtMYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target with AtMYB12 to enhance precursor supply for phenylpropanoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endogenous promoter; activated by E8-driven AtMYB12

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form DAHP, initiating the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive polyphenols with nutritional and health-related value
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Induced in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in WT peel relative to flesh
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances carbon flow into shikimate and aromatic amino acid biosynthesis, supporting phenylpropanoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; isotope-label flux analysis through AtMYB12 activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlDAHPS is upregulated in AtMYB12 tomato fruit. 2. AtMYB12 directly binds the SlDAHPS promoter. 3. SlMYB12 binds the same promoter region in WT peel. 4. SlDAHPS is proposed as a key determinant of carbon flow into shikimate-derived phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key determinant of carbon flow into shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening; AtMYB12 expression; glucose isotope feeding
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specifically emphasized by authors as a direct target explaining enhanced aromatic amino acid supply.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to boost shikimate-derived nutritional metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endogenous promoter; activated by E8-driven AtMYB12

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonol branch competing with isoflavone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to form dihydroflavonols
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogenic compounds with reported health relevance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Functional deficiency in are mutant used in fruit engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced F3H activity decreases flavonol formation and redirects naringenin toward isoflavone accumulation, strongly increasing genistin/isoflavones in AtMYB12 backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic mutant combination (are-/-) with E8:LjIFS and AtMYB12; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The are mutant is deficient in F3H activity. 2. Introducing are into AtMYB12 reduces flavonol accumulation to ~30% of AtMYB12 levels. 3. In LjIFS-expressing fruit, the are mutation greatly increases total isoflavones by reducing competition for naringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening; presence of AtMYB12 and E8:LjIFS transgenes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses a naturally occurring mutant rather than direct transgenic overexpression of SlF3H; included because it is a core experimentally manipulated study-species pathway gene in Results.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful knockout/editing target to reroute flux from flavonols to isoflavones in stacked engineering strategies

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoalexin with reported therapeutic and lifespan-extending bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in tomato fruit under CaMV 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces resveratrol in tomato fruit, and with AtMYB12 raises total resveratrol derivatives to 5–6 mg g^-1 DW
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; genetic crossing with AtMYB12; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 35S:VvStSy tomato accumulates ~0.5 mg g^-1 DW trans-resveratrol. 2. Co-expression with AtMYB12 boosts resveratrol and derivatives to 5–6 mg g^-1 DW. 3. Stilbene synthase effectively competes with chalcone synthase, reducing flavonol accumulation in the stacked line.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core engineering gene for novel phenylpropanoid production, but origin species is grape while study species is tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduces flavonol accumulation due to competition with CHS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transgene for tomato-based resveratrol biofortification when stacked with AtMYB12

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones such as naringenin to isoflavones such as genistein
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogenic compounds with proposed benefits and risks in hormone-related diseases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in tomato fruit under E8 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables genistin accumulation in tomato fruit; co-expression with AtMYB12 and reduction of F3H strongly increase isoflavone levels up to ~78 mg g^-1 DW
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; crossing with AtMYB12 are-/- line; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E8:LjIFS alone produces low levels of genistin in tomato fruit. 2. Addition of AtMYB12 significantly increases genistin production. 3. Combining LjIFS with AtMYB12 and are-/- massively boosts total isoflavones by reducing competition from F3H.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening; AtMYB12 co-expression; are mutant background
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core engineering gene for novel phenylpropanoid biosynthesis in tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competes with endogenous flavonoid branch; health impacts of dietary isoflavones may be context dependent
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for tomato isoflavone biofortification, especially with AtMYB12 stacking and F3H reduction

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in tomato fruit under the fruit-specific E8 promoter; strongly induced at 3–4 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlENO; SlDAHPS; PAL5A; PAL5C; PAL5D; CHS1; F3H
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates genes of glycolysis, pentose phosphate pathway, shikimate pathway, and flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Precursor supply, aromatic amino acid biosynthesis, and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Binds promoters containing TACCTACC motif; functional homologue SlMYB12 shares targets
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols; hydroxycinnamates; chlorogenic acid; anthocyanins; resveratrol derivatives; isoflavones
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary bioactives with potential health-promoting properties
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases phenylpropanoid accumulation and enables much higher production of engineered stilbenes and isoflavones by boosting both demand and precursor supply
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic fruit-specific expression; RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; 14C/13C glucose feeding and flux analysis; metabolite profiling; genetic crosses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fruit-specific AtMYB12 increases expression of genes in both primary and secondary metabolism. 2. It directly binds promoters of SlENO and SlDAHPS, in addition to phenylpropanoid genes PAL5A/PAL5C/PAL5D/CHS1/F3H. 3. It increases carbon flux through glycolysis, OPPP, TCA cycle and shikimate-derived aromatic amino acid production. 4. It greatly enhances accumulation of flavonols and hydroxycinnamates and boosts engineered anthocyanin, resveratrol and isoflavone production when stacked with other genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator that controls both supply and demand for phenylpropanoid production
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Authors discuss possible metabolite repression under constitutive expression in photosynthetic tissues; not directly established in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; E8-driven expression in fruit; isotope-labeled glucose feeding
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE61014; ChIP-seq: GEO GSE62462
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central gene of the study. Compared with Del/Ros1, AtMYB12 uniquely activates primary metabolism in addition to specialized metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive 35S-driven AtMYB12 in tobacco reduced growth; E8 fruit-specific expression is more effective and may avoid broader growth penalties
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent general tool for metabolic engineering and biofortification of shikimate/phenylpropanoid-derived compounds in fruit; best used in fruit-specific stacking strategies

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in transgenic tomato fruit in Del/Ros1 purple line
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (late flavonoid biosynthetic genes)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Mainly activates genes encoding enzymes late in flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with Ros1 in anthocyanin induction
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigments with antioxidant and dietary value
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit, and with AtMYB12 yields Indigo fruit with further increased anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic cross with AtMYB12 line; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Del/Ros1 drives strong anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit. 2. Del/Ros1 mainly activates late anthocyanin genes rather than primary metabolism. 3. Crossing with AtMYB12 further increases anthocyanins and other phenylpropanoids in Indigo fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-specific activator of anthocyanin branch, but less broad than AtMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used comparatively to show AtMYB12 differs by activating precursor supply pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not increase aromatic amino acid supply
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking regulator for anthocyanin-rich fruit, especially combined with AtMYB12

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in transgenic tomato fruit in Del/Ros1 purple line
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (late flavonoid biosynthetic genes)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Mainly activates genes encoding enzymes late in flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with Del in anthocyanin induction
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigments with antioxidant and dietary value
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit, and with AtMYB12 contributes to maximal anthocyanin accumulation in Indigo fruit
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic cross with AtMYB12 line; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ros1 with Del induces anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit. 2. Del/Ros1 mainly upregulates anthocyanin-pathway genes rather than primary metabolism genes. 3. Combined with AtMYB12 it contributes to broader and higher phenylpropanoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Anthocyanin-branch activator in the Del/Ros1 module
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally used regulator in Results, although not the primary mechanistic focus.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not enhance precursor supply from primary metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for anthocyanin biofortification when stacked with AtMYB12
